description: Editable CaiLama model-role benchmark task catalog. The runner executes these tasks role by role for each candidate model. Prompts are privacy-safe and contain no productive secrets or private raw PGN.

tasks.label: Route PGN analysis
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Analyze benchmark-three-games.pgn, classify all moves, deeply analyze key positions, and prepare feedback cases.
tasks.expected_task: Choose role task and include the PGN analysis tools in the tools list.
tasks.quality_question: Is the routing JSON correct, concise, and usable?

tasks.label: Route DWZ lookup
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Find current DWZ context for a named opponent before preparing training advice.
tasks.expected_task: Choose role researcher or task and include search_dwz.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router select the search-capable path without inventing data?

tasks.label: Route FIDE lookup
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Check FIDE rating context for a player and cite the source before analysis.
tasks.expected_task: Choose role researcher or task and include search_fide.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router request the correct external-rating tool path?

tasks.label: Route OCR diagram
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Extract a chess diagram from a PDF page, verify the FEN, and report uncertainty.
tasks.expected_task: Choose role vision or task and include analyze_ocr_document.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router avoid analysis first and route through OCR verification?

tasks.label: Route opening explanation
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Explain the opening idea behind 1.e4 c6 2.d4 d5 3.Nc3 dxe4.
tasks.expected_task: Choose a chess analysis role and include get_opening_text when opening text is useful.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router attach opening context instead of guessing?

tasks.label: Route RAG research
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Answer using the project documentation and cite the exact source snippets.
tasks.expected_task: Choose researcher and include search_rag.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router select RAG with citation intent?

tasks.label: Route engine eval
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: For FEN r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/4p3/2B1P3/5N2/PPPP1PPP/RNBQK2R b KQkq - 2 3, get the current engine evaluation.
tasks.expected_task: Choose a task or analyst path and include get_position_eval.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router ask for board truth before verbal analysis?

tasks.label: Route coach session
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Start a short training session from an existing weighted training position, ask me to set it up on the DGT board if connected, and keep a Unicode board fallback.
tasks.expected_task: Choose coach and no destructive/live tool unless explicitly available.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router keep the bounded coach-session intent?

tasks.label: Route pure translation
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Translate this chess paragraph to German and keep SAN notation unchanged.
tasks.expected_task: Choose translator with no chess-analysis tools.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router avoid unnecessary analysis tools for translation?

tasks.label: Route profile import
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only. User request: Import this already approved benchmark game into a player profile and derive weighted training positions.
tasks.expected_task: Choose task and include profile_game, classify_moves and analyze_moves.
tasks.quality_question: Does the router plan profile import and analysis with the correct tool family?

tasks.label: Legal move tags
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 2. Candidate moves: Nf3, Bc4, Qh5, d4. Return JSON array with move, tags, quality_band.
tasks.expected_task: Classify every candidate with tags and a quality_band.
tasks.quality_question: Are the move tags plausible and complete enough?

tasks.label: Check detection
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: 4k3/8/8/8/8/8/4Q3/4K3 w - - 0 1. Candidate moves: Qe7, Qa8, Qb7. Return JSON array with tags including check where applicable.
tasks.expected_task: Mark checking moves and give concise quality bands.
tasks.quality_question: Does the classifier identify checks without over-explaining?

tasks.label: Promotion tags
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: 4k3/P7/8/8/8/8/8/4K3 w - - 0 1. Candidate moves: a8=Q, a8=N, Kd2. Return JSON array with tags and quality_band.
tasks.expected_task: Tag promotions and distinguish legal non-promotion moves.
tasks.quality_question: Are promotions recognized and not flattened into generic quiet moves?

tasks.label: Development tags
tasks.prompt: Opening position after 1.e4 e5. Candidate moves: Nf3, Bc4, h4, Ke2. Return JSON array with move, tags, quality_band.
tasks.expected_task: Tag development and suspicious king moves.
tasks.quality_question: Does it separate development from weakening moves?

tasks.label: Protects piece
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/3NP3/8/PPP2PPP/RNBQKB1R b KQkq - 1 2. Candidate moves: Nf6, d6, Qe7. Return JSON array with tags including protects_piece where useful.
tasks.expected_task: Tag defensive/protective moves when the move adds protection.
tasks.quality_question: Are protective moves tagged conservatively?

tasks.label: Double attack
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: r3k2r/ppp2ppp/2n5/3q4/8/2N2N2/PPP2PPP/R2QK2R w KQkq - 0 1. Candidate moves: Nxd5, O-O, Qe2. Return JSON array with tactical tags.
tasks.expected_task: Flag possible double attacks only if justified by board facts.
tasks.quality_question: Does the model avoid tactical overclaiming?

tasks.label: Discovery attack
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: 4k3/8/8/8/4R3/4B3/8/4K3 w - - 0 1. Candidate moves: Bf4, Bd4, Re8. Return JSON array with tags including discovery_attack if applicable.
tasks.expected_task: Identify discovery-related motifs only when the move opens a line.
tasks.quality_question: Is discovery-attack tagging restrained and logical?

tasks.label: Moves out of attack
tasks.prompt: Position FEN: 4k3/8/8/8/8/8/4r3/4K1Q1 w - - 0 1. Candidate moves: Kf1, Kd1, Qxe2. Return JSON array with tags including moves_out_of_attack when applicable.
tasks.expected_task: Mark escaping attacked squares and captures.
tasks.quality_question: Are evasions and captures classified coherently?

tasks.label: Quality bands only
tasks.prompt: Given candidate labels from an engine precheck: A improves from -3.2 to +0.4, B stays near 0.0, C drops from +1.2 to -5.0. Return JSON array with id and quality_band from loosing-blunder, blunder, mistake, ungenau, okay, good, stark, brilliant.
tasks.expected_task: Map score swings to CaiLama quality bands.
tasks.quality_question: Are the quality bands consistent with the score swings?

tasks.label: Multi-tag compression
tasks.prompt: Candidate move summary: Nf7+ forks king and queen; Qe2 defends e4; h3 is quiet luft; e8=Q promotes with check. Return JSON array with move, tags, quality_band.
tasks.expected_task: Assign multiple tags when appropriate.
tasks.quality_question: Does it support multi-label classification without losing the main tactic?

tasks.label: Deep position review
tasks.prompt: Analyze FEN r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/4p3/2B1P3/5N2/PPPP1PPP/RNBQK2R b KQkq - 2 3. Separate board facts, candidate plans, risks, and uncertainty.
tasks.expected_task: Produce a grounded English analysis without invented engine lines.
tasks.quality_question: Is the strategic analysis useful and logically grounded?

tasks.label: Training value
tasks.prompt: A player repeatedly castles late and misses king-safety tactics. Explain the training value of one key position and propose two themes.
tasks.expected_task: Connect analysis to training themes and avoid generic advice.
tasks.quality_question: Are the training themes concrete enough to become weighted positions?

tasks.label: Candidate line uncertainty
tasks.prompt: Given no engine output, compare candidate moves Nf3, d4, Bc4 for an Italian-game structure and state what cannot be concluded.
tasks.expected_task: Explain without pretending engine certainty.
tasks.quality_question: Does it mark uncertainty instead of overclaiming?

tasks.label: Conversion plan
tasks.prompt: A side has an extra rook but exposed king. Give a conversion plan with risk controls and one critical tactical check to verify.
tasks.expected_task: Offer practical plan and tactical verification point.
tasks.quality_question: Is the plan practical rather than just material-counting?

tasks.label: Opening-to-middlegame bridge
tasks.prompt: Explain how a Caro-Kann Advance structure can lead to a training question about pawn breaks, not memorized moves.
tasks.expected_task: Bridge opening idea to middlegame training.
tasks.quality_question: Does it avoid rote opening theory and focus on transferable plans?

tasks.label: Endgame logic
tasks.prompt: Analyze a rook ending where one side has an outside passer but passive rook. List principles, candidate plan, and one trap.
tasks.expected_task: Give endgame reasoning with uncertainty.
tasks.quality_question: Are endgame principles applied concretely?

tasks.label: Blunder explanation
tasks.prompt: Explain why a natural developing move can be a blunder if it leaves a back-rank tactic. Include how to teach the pattern.
tasks.expected_task: Connect tactic, cause, and coaching use.
tasks.quality_question: Is the causal explanation clear and teachable?

tasks.label: Sparse evidence
tasks.prompt: You only know: White missed a defensive resource, engine swing was large, exact line unknown. Write a safe analysis note.
tasks.expected_task: Use sparse facts and clearly label missing evidence.
tasks.quality_question: Does it stay useful without fabricating details?

tasks.label: Plan comparison
tasks.prompt: Compare two plans in a queenless middlegame: improve worst piece versus push passed pawn. Give evaluation factors and decision trigger.
tasks.expected_task: Compare plans with concrete trigger conditions.
tasks.quality_question: Are the decision criteria chess-relevant?

tasks.label: Benchmark critique
tasks.prompt: Review a model answer that says 'this move is brilliant because it wins'. Explain why that is insufficient for CaiLama feedback.
tasks.expected_task: Critique shallow praise and suggest stronger evidence.
tasks.quality_question: Does it improve benchmark evaluation criteria?

tasks.label: Safe benchmark plan
tasks.prompt: Plan a CaiLama benchmark run: verify router, run each cloud model through role tasks, upload every 50 observations, update docs. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Return a small ordered plan only.
tasks.quality_question: Is the plan actionable and safe?

tasks.label: Repo boundary plan
tasks.prompt: Plan changes across Master, CaiLama, Router, and Search without committing subrepo files in Master. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Respect repo boundaries and verification steps.
tasks.quality_question: Does the plan preserve the ecosystem rules?

tasks.label: Tool plan load PGN
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only for a safe tool plan to load benchmark-three-games.pgn and classify moves.
tasks.expected_task: Emit a tool plan with load_pgn and classify_moves.
tasks.quality_question: Is the tool plan structurally correct?

tasks.label: Retry policy
tasks.prompt: Define the runtime behavior when the router returns HTTP 500 for a cloud model during benchmark. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Describe three attempts with wait time and secret-free error export.
tasks.quality_question: Is the retry policy robust without hiding failures?

tasks.label: Feedback processing
tasks.prompt: Plan how to process human feedback after blind review and reassign best models per role. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Return steps for aggregating feedback and updating role assignments.
tasks.quality_question: Does it connect feedback to model assignment?

tasks.label: Prompt deploy plan
tasks.prompt: Plan how Master prompt templates should be copied into runtime without secrets. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Return deploy-safe steps and verification.
tasks.quality_question: Is versioned-prompt deployment clear?

tasks.label: Unexpected tool review
tasks.prompt: A model called search_rag although no tool was expected. Plan the validation and human feedback flow. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Validate structure automatically and request human judgment on usefulness.
tasks.quality_question: Does it separate structural validation from semantic judgment?

tasks.label: Database reset plan
tasks.prompt: Plan resetting website benchmark tables, provider observations, and CaiLama runtime DB before a clean run. Do not print secrets.
tasks.expected_task: Return safe reset checklist without credentials.
tasks.quality_question: Is it complete without leaking credentials?

tasks.label: Concurrency plan
tasks.prompt: Plan cloud concurrency with two router backends capped at three in-flight requests each. Local models are excluded. Do not execute tools.
tasks.expected_task: Mention backend caps and benchmark-level sequencing.
tasks.quality_question: Does the plan avoid overloading a single account?

tasks.label: PTG gate plan
tasks.prompt: Plan the rule: run PTG only after every role has a tested assigned model, and skip PTG if a new model changes no assignment.
tasks.expected_task: Return concise gate logic and summary fields.
tasks.quality_question: Is the PTG gate understandable and implementable?

tasks.label: Translate with glossary 1
tasks.prompt: Glossary: blunder=Patzer; inaccuracy=Ungenauigkeit; candidate move=Kandidatenzug. Translate to German: The candidate move avoids a blunder but still leaves an inaccuracy.
tasks.expected_task: Translate into German and preserve chess meaning using glossary terms.
tasks.quality_question: Ist die Übersetzung fachlich präzise und nutzt sie das Wörterbuch?

tasks.label: Translate without glossary 1
tasks.prompt: Translate to German: The candidate move avoids a blunder but still leaves an inaccuracy.
tasks.expected_task: Translate into German with standard chess terminology.
tasks.quality_question: Ist die Übersetzung ohne Wörterbuch idiomatisch?

tasks.label: Translate with glossary 2
tasks.prompt: Glossary: forcing line=forcierte Variante; defensive resource=Verteidigungsressource; rook ending=Turmendspiel. Translate to German: White missed a forcing defensive resource and entered a worse rook ending.
tasks.expected_task: Translate into German and keep the technical terms aligned with the glossary.
tasks.quality_question: Werden die Glossarbegriffe korrekt verwendet?

tasks.label: Translate without glossary 2
tasks.prompt: Translate to German: White missed a forcing defensive resource and entered a worse rook ending.
tasks.expected_task: Translate into German with natural chess language.
tasks.quality_question: Ist die Übersetzung natürlich und korrekt?

tasks.label: Preserve notation
tasks.prompt: Glossary: kingside=Königsflügel; queenside=Damenflügel. Translate to German and preserve SAN: After 18.Nxf7 Kxf7 19.Qh5+, Black must defend the kingside.
tasks.expected_task: Translate while preserving 18.Nxf7 Kxf7 19.Qh5+.
tasks.quality_question: Bleibt die Notation unverändert?

tasks.label: Engine score text
tasks.prompt: Translate to German: The engine swing from +1.2 to -3.8 marks a blunder, but the human explanation should stay cautious.
tasks.expected_task: Translate and preserve numeric scores.
tasks.quality_question: Sind Bewertung und Vorsicht korrekt übertragen?

tasks.label: Coach wording
tasks.prompt: Translate to German: Set up the position on the board if your DGT board is connected. The Unicode board is shown below as fallback.
tasks.expected_task: Translate into clear user-facing German.
tasks.quality_question: Klingt der Text in der Oberfläche gut verständlich?

tasks.label: RAG provenance wording
tasks.prompt: Glossary: source snippet=Quellenauszug; citation=Quellenangabe. Translate to German: The answer must cite both source snippets and separate facts from inference.
tasks.expected_task: Use glossary and preserve provenance meaning.
tasks.quality_question: Werden Quellenbezug und Schlussfolgerung sauber übertragen?

tasks.label: Short UI label
tasks.prompt: Translate to German for a compact button label: Review next unscored benchmark case.
tasks.expected_task: Return a short German UI label only.
tasks.quality_question: Ist das Label kurz und treffend?

tasks.label: Quality bands
tasks.prompt: Translate these quality bands for German UI labels: loosing-blunder, blunder, mistake, inaccuracy, okay, good, strong, brilliant.
tasks.expected_task: Return a mapping from English key to German label.
tasks.quality_question: Sind die Qualitätsbänder verständlich und konsistent?

tasks.label: Bounded training prompt
tasks.prompt: Create one training question for FEN 4k3/8/8/8/8/8/4Q3/4K3 w - - 0 1. Include setup instruction for DGT if connected and a Unicode-board fallback sentence.
tasks.expected_task: Ask a spoiler-light question and keep the session bounded.
tasks.quality_question: Is the coach question clear without revealing too much?

tasks.label: Hint ladder
tasks.prompt: Create a three-step hint ladder for a tactic where Nf7+ forks king and queen. Do not reveal the final move in hint 1.
tasks.expected_task: Return three hints with increasing specificity.
tasks.quality_question: Is the hint ladder pedagogically useful?

tasks.label: After answer follow-up
tasks.prompt: A student chose a move tagged mistake. Ask one follow-up question that helps them find the missed defensive resource.
tasks.expected_task: Ask one focused follow-up question.
tasks.quality_question: Does the question guide without scolding?

tasks.label: Abort or complete
tasks.prompt: Write a console prompt asking whether the current training session should be completed with follow-up analysis or aborted.
tasks.expected_task: Offer exactly two clear choices.
tasks.quality_question: Is the session control unambiguous?

tasks.label: Weighted position intro
tasks.prompt: Explain to a learner why this weighted training position was selected today: recurring back-rank weakness and recent mistake pattern.
tasks.expected_task: Give concise personalized rationale.
tasks.quality_question: Does it connect profile evidence to the task?

tasks.label: No engine spoiler
tasks.prompt: Create a training question from an engine-found swing without revealing engine score or best move.
tasks.expected_task: Hide score and best move while making the task actionable.
tasks.quality_question: Does it avoid spoilers and still focus attention?

tasks.label: Board setup sanity
tasks.prompt: Ask the user to set up a position on a physical board and confirm side to move before answering.
tasks.expected_task: Mention board setup and side-to-move confirmation.
tasks.quality_question: Is the board-flow instruction practical?

tasks.label: Pattern transfer
tasks.prompt: Turn the theme 'piece pinned to queen' into a reusable training takeaway after the exercise.
tasks.expected_task: Return a short transfer lesson.
tasks.quality_question: Is the takeaway transferable beyond the exact position?

tasks.label: Confidence repair
tasks.prompt: The learner missed a simple tactic. Write a factual, non-patronizing response with one next action.
tasks.expected_task: Keep tone respectful and focused.
tasks.quality_question: Is the tone useful and not demotivating?

tasks.label: Session summary
tasks.prompt: Summarize a completed session with result: found tactic after hint 2; missed defensive resource; next theme back-rank safety.
tasks.expected_task: Return compact summary and next training suggestion.
tasks.quality_question: Does it summarize outcome and next step cleanly?

tasks.label: Grounded eval note
tasks.prompt: Engine precheck: FEN 4k3/8/8/8/8/8/4Q3/4K3 w - - 0 1, best move Qe7#, eval mate. Write analysis with facts, candidate moves, uncertainty.
tasks.expected_task: Use given engine fact and do not add unsupported lines.
tasks.quality_question: Does the analysis stay grounded in supplied facts?

tasks.label: Legal move details
tasks.prompt: Given legal moves Nf3, Bc4, d4 and candidate tags from board truth, explain which details should be shown in a benchmark artifact.
tasks.expected_task: Explain legal-move provenance and useful summary fields.
tasks.quality_question: Does it identify useful artifact fields?

tasks.label: Outlier precheck
tasks.prompt: A model rates a forced mate as only okay. Explain how automatic precheck should flag this before human feedback.
tasks.expected_task: Describe outlier flagging without replacing human quality review.
tasks.quality_question: Is the auto-precheck idea correct and limited?

tasks.label: Training position weight
tasks.prompt: Explain how to assign weight to a training position based on frequency, severity, recency, and user feedback.
tasks.expected_task: Provide a structured weighting rationale.
tasks.quality_question: Is the weighting logic defensible?

tasks.label: PTG key positions
tasks.prompt: Explain why 21 key positions from three example games must not become a product-wide maximum.
tasks.expected_task: State benchmark observation versus product default.
tasks.quality_question: Does it preserve the distinction correctly?

tasks.label: Move quality scale
tasks.prompt: Map engine swing and tactical facts into bands loosing-blunder, blunder, mistake, ungenau, okay, good, stark, brilliant.
tasks.expected_task: Describe scale with caveats.
tasks.quality_question: Is the scale usable without false precision?

tasks.label: RAG provenance
tasks.prompt: Explain how an analysis should quote project docs using source snippets without leaking internal paths.
tasks.expected_task: Use provenance and privacy constraints.
tasks.quality_question: Does it handle source grounding and privacy?

tasks.label: Coach handoff
tasks.prompt: Convert a factual analysis into what the coach needs: position, question, expected motif, DGT setup flag, Unicode board fallback.
tasks.expected_task: List handoff fields and rationale.
tasks.quality_question: Does it bridge analysis to training correctly?

tasks.label: Ambiguous tactic
tasks.prompt: Two candidate moves both look tactical, but only one has engine support. Explain how to write uncertainty in benchmark output.
tasks.expected_task: Mark evidence levels and avoid overclaiming.
tasks.quality_question: Is uncertainty wording precise?

tasks.label: Quality gate
tasks.prompt: Design a quality gate for accepting a model answer in role analyst before it reaches human blind feedback.
tasks.expected_task: List deterministic checks and human-review fields.
tasks.quality_question: Is the gate strict where objective and open where subjective?

tasks.label: Unsupported engine claim
tasks.prompt: Critique: 'Stockfish proves Qh5 is brilliant' although no engine output is provided. Identify faults and safer wording.
tasks.expected_task: Find unsupported claim and correction.
tasks.quality_question: Does the critique catch hallucinated engine authority?

tasks.label: Tool misuse
tasks.prompt: Critique a benchmark response that calls search_rag for a pure translation task. Decide what is structural failure and what needs human review.
tasks.expected_task: Separate wrong tool from semantic value.
tasks.quality_question: Does it distinguish automatic and human feedback?

tasks.label: Overlong feedback page
tasks.prompt: Critique a feedback UI that shows model name, usage tier, full prompt, and long answer before scoring.
tasks.expected_task: Identify blind-study bias and usability issues.
tasks.quality_question: Does it protect blind evaluation?

tasks.label: Bad German translation
tasks.prompt: Critique translation: 'The rook ending is won' -> 'Das Turm-Ende ist gewonnen'. Explain better term.
tasks.expected_task: Identify terminology error and fix.
tasks.quality_question: Does it improve chess German?

tasks.label: Missing legal moves
tasks.prompt: Critique an analysis artifact that gives move scores but no legal-move tags or board truth fields.
tasks.expected_task: Explain missing evidence for downstream agents.
tasks.quality_question: Does it name concrete missing fields?

tasks.label: Retry failure
tasks.prompt: Critique a benchmark runner that marks a model failed after one router HTTP 500.
tasks.expected_task: Recommend three retries with wait and secret-free error export.
tasks.quality_question: Does it catch transient-provider risk?

tasks.label: PTG too early
tasks.prompt: Critique starting the PTG run before every role has a tested assigned model.
tasks.expected_task: Explain why the gate is required.
tasks.quality_question: Does it align with the new benchmark flow?

tasks.label: Private path leak
tasks.prompt: Critique an observation export containing /srv/schach/mytraining/processed/ollama_import.pgn.
tasks.expected_task: Flag privacy leak and propose safe label.
tasks.quality_question: Does it catch local path leakage?

tasks.label: Shallow coach hint
tasks.prompt: Critique coach hint: 'Find the best move.' Improve it without revealing the solution.
tasks.expected_task: Explain why vague hints fail and rewrite.
tasks.quality_question: Is the replacement actually more helpful?

tasks.label: Bad assignment metric
tasks.prompt: Critique choosing best model only by speed when content quality differs strongly.
tasks.expected_task: Explain multi-factor scoring.
tasks.quality_question: Does it balance cost, speed, correctness and feedback?

tasks.label: Uncertain FEN
tasks.prompt: OCR says: diagram detected, white to move, c-file pieces unclear, two back-rank squares uncertain. Should a FEN be emitted?
tasks.expected_task: Do not emit FEN; explain uncertainty and verification step.
tasks.quality_question: Does it avoid guessing board truth?

tasks.label: Confident squares
tasks.prompt: Diagram parse: kings confirmed e1/e8, queens confirmed d1/d8, all pawns uncertain. Produce a safe extraction note.
tasks.expected_task: List confirmed and uncertain data only.
tasks.quality_question: Is the uncertainty model usable?

tasks.label: OCR false positive
tasks.prompt: Text page contains 'diagram' but no board grid was detected. Decide whether to create a diagram candidate.
tasks.expected_task: Reject false positive and explain gate.
tasks.quality_question: Does it reduce OCR false positives?

tasks.label: Board orientation
tasks.prompt: A diagram has coordinates from Black's side but text says White to move. What metadata must be stored before FEN reconstruction?
tasks.expected_task: Mention orientation and side-to-move evidence.
tasks.quality_question: Does it capture orientation risk?

tasks.label: Piece ambiguity
tasks.prompt: OCR confidence: e4 white knight 0.51 or bishop 0.49. Write a verification note for human review.
tasks.expected_task: Represent ambiguity without deciding.
tasks.quality_question: Is the ambiguity clearly expressed?

tasks.label: Text-to-board link
tasks.prompt: A PDF paragraph references 'Diagram 3, Black to move'. Explain how to link text and board candidate safely.
tasks.expected_task: Describe linkage and confidence fields.
tasks.quality_question: Does it preserve provenance?

tasks.label: FEN validation failure
tasks.prompt: A reconstructed FEN has nine white pawns. What should the pipeline do?
tasks.expected_task: Reject FEN and create diagnostic feedback case.
tasks.quality_question: Does it catch illegal reconstruction?

tasks.label: Clean FEN pass
tasks.prompt: All pieces are high confidence and FEN validates. What should be exported to benchmark feedback?
tasks.expected_task: Export FEN, side to move, confidence and source label.
tasks.quality_question: Are the useful fields included?

tasks.label: No image available
tasks.prompt: A vision model is asked to inspect a diagram but no image bytes are attached. Respond safely.
tasks.expected_task: State missing input and request image/metadata.
tasks.quality_question: Does it avoid hallucinating an unseen board?

tasks.label: Diagram quality gate
tasks.prompt: Design a deterministic gate for OCR/FEN before an LLM explains the position.
tasks.expected_task: List objective checks before LLM analysis.
tasks.quality_question: Are the gates concrete and practical?

tasks.label: Training card
tasks.prompt: Create a privacy-safe training card from: motif back-rank weakness, side White, prompt 'Find the defensive resource', source benchmark case A.
tasks.expected_task: Return title, setup, question, motif, source label, and uncertainty.
tasks.quality_question: Is the card usable without leaking private data?

tasks.label: Session record
tasks.prompt: Record a completed session: found after hint 2, missed first candidate, next theme pins. Keep it concise.
tasks.expected_task: Return structured session note.
tasks.quality_question: Is the session record concise and useful?

tasks.label: Feedback case summary
tasks.prompt: Summarize a benchmark observation for human blind feedback: role analyst, task outlier precheck, error_status empty, output excerpt available.
tasks.expected_task: Keep enough context but hide model identity and usage.
tasks.quality_question: Is the summary sparse but sufficient?

tasks.label: Tool-call note
tasks.prompt: Document unexpected tool call search_rag in a benchmark answer. Include structural status and human review question.
tasks.expected_task: Create human feedback note for unexpected tool usefulness.
tasks.quality_question: Does it make the human decision easy?

tasks.label: Artifact provenance
tasks.prompt: Write a one-paragraph artifact note explaining that raw PGN stays local while observations are sanitized.
tasks.expected_task: State privacy policy clearly.
tasks.quality_question: Is the privacy explanation accurate?

tasks.label: Prompt version note
tasks.prompt: Create a changelog entry for updated benchmark prompt templates copied from Master into runtime.
tasks.expected_task: Mention versioned Master source and runtime copy.
tasks.quality_question: Is the changelog clear?

tasks.label: Role assignment summary
tasks.prompt: Write a summary after a model tested all roles: wins translator, loses analyst, no PTG change. Keep it factual.
tasks.expected_task: Summarize role results and PTG gate outcome.
tasks.quality_question: Is the assignment status clear?

tasks.label: German UI text
tasks.prompt: Write concise German UI text for 'No position stored for this benchmark case'.
tasks.expected_task: Return one German sentence.
tasks.quality_question: Ist der UI-Text klar und kurz?

tasks.label: Manual feedback export
tasks.prompt: Prepare a note that free-text human feedback will be stored with context for later Codex processing, without model names.
tasks.expected_task: State blind feedback handling.
tasks.quality_question: Does it preserve blind-study constraints?

tasks.label: Benchmark final line
tasks.prompt: Write a final CLI line after uploading 50 observations successfully. Include count and next action, no secrets.
tasks.expected_task: Return one concise CLI status line.
tasks.quality_question: Is the status useful during long runs?

tasks.label: Cite two sources
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] Router owns model aliases. [2] Master owns website and benchmark tasks. Answer: where should prompt templates live? Cite both.
tasks.expected_task: Answer using only sources and citations [1], [2].
tasks.quality_question: Does it cite correctly and avoid extra claims?

tasks.label: Fact versus inference
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] Local models are excluded for the current run. [2] Cloud backends are capped at three in-flight requests each. Explain what is fact and what is inference.
tasks.expected_task: Separate facts from inference with citations.
tasks.quality_question: Is provenance clear?

tasks.label: No source answer
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] CaiLama uses MariaDB for runtime DB. Question: what is the production password?
tasks.expected_task: Refuse unsupported secret request and cite source limits.
tasks.quality_question: Does it refuse secrets and avoid hallucinating?

tasks.label: Search tool plan
tasks.prompt: Return JSON only for a RAG lookup about benchmark feedback schema fields.
tasks.expected_task: Include search_rag in a tool plan.
tasks.quality_question: Is the RAG tool plan valid?

tasks.label: DWZ source warning
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] DWZ data must be staged and verified before semantic release. Explain why a live claim should be cautious.
tasks.expected_task: Give cautious answer with source citation.
tasks.quality_question: Does it avoid overclaiming unstaged data?

tasks.label: Website feature summary
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] Feedback is blind. [2] Usage level is hidden during feedback but stored for internal scoring. Summarize user-facing behavior.
tasks.expected_task: Summarize with citations and no model names.
tasks.quality_question: Does it preserve blind-study constraints?

tasks.label: Runtime path privacy
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] Runtime paths stay outside docs. [2] Versioned prompts live in Master. Explain deployment documentation boundaries.
tasks.expected_task: Cite both and avoid hard local paths.
tasks.quality_question: Does it avoid local path leakage?

tasks.label: Model capability uncertainty
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] If a model/mode is not proven available, mark it unsure. Explain benchmark inventory policy.
tasks.expected_task: State defensive policy with citation.
tasks.quality_question: Does it handle uncertainty honestly?

tasks.label: Feedback aggregation
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] Human feedback can re-sort role assignments. [2] Free text is stored for later Codex processing. Summarize processing flow.
tasks.expected_task: Cite both and keep blind-study identity hidden.
tasks.quality_question: Is the flow easy to understand?

tasks.label: PTG gate source
tasks.prompt: Sources: [1] PTG starts only when every role has a tested assigned model. [2] If a new model changes no assignment, do not rerun PTG. Explain the gate.
tasks.expected_task: Explain gate with both citations.
tasks.quality_question: Does it capture the exact PTG rule?
